behaviors.description: The scoring model assigns the correct risk score when a transaction exceeds the configured amount threshold for its merchant category.
behaviors.justification: The core scoring math can be unit-tested, but this behavior specifically depends on wiring between rule configuration, merchant-category lookup, and scoring aggregation. That module handoff is where unit-only confidence can be false. I considered Unit, but the behavior text says "configured ... for its merchant category," which requires config+category integration rather than a single isolated function. I rejected System because the behavior does not require real HTTP/subprocess/OS or an explicit external contract/round-trip side effect.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scoring service with real in-process config loading and merchant-category mapping modules (no network). Feed a transaction over that category’s configured threshold and assert the produced numeric score matches expected weighted rule contribution.

behaviors.description: The risk model identifies a fraudulent transaction pattern (e.g., rapid small purchases from different geographies) even when no single rule threshold is exceeded.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real AI/ML model judgment (fraud pattern recognition quality), which stubs cannot validate. This is exactly the "model classifies patterns accurately" case. I considered Integration, but rejected it because the behavior is about identification quality, not only routing/retries/payload plumbing. I rejected Workflow because only one model decision is required, not multiple agentic steps chained with cross-step state dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real risk model on representative transaction sequences where rule thresholds stay below scoring triggers, and assert the model assigns fraud likelihood above the review threshold for known-fraud patterns while controls remain below threshold.

behaviors.description: The velocity check in the scoring model correctly counts the number of transactions within the configured time window per user.
behaviors.justification: The failure risk is in module interaction between transaction retrieval/filtering, window configuration, user scoping, and time-bound counting logic. Unit tests on counting logic alone can miss handoff defects. I considered Unit, but rejected it because the behavior includes "configured time window per user," indicating cross-module state/query wiring. I rejected System because no explicit real external contract, real network, or OS-only behavior is required by the text.
behaviors.test_description: Use scoring service + repository layer against a local test datastore/fixture set with boundary timestamps; assert per-user velocity counts are correct inside/outside the configured window and that those counts feed the score correctly.

behaviors.description: A transaction flagged by the risk model but not by the scoring model is still held for review — the models operate as an OR gate, not AND.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state-handoff logic across two model outputs and review-decision logic; unit tests per component can miss combination defects. Integration is sufficient because correctness here does not depend on real model output quality (inputs can be controlled flags) and no explicit real external contract/round-trip requirement is stated. I considered Agentic but rejected it: behavior text is about OR gating semantics, not whether the risk model makes correct fraud judgments. I considered System but rejected it because "held for review" is not explicitly tied to a required real external API/UI/readback contract in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In an integrated service test, inject model results where risk=true and score=false, execute decisioning flow, and assert review_hold=true; include converse cases to verify OR semantics and guard against accidental AND logic.

behaviors.description: The combined output includes both the numeric risk score and the risk model's fraud probability, so the reviewer can see which model flagged the transaction and why.
behaviors.justification: The main risk is schema/assembly wiring across scoring output, risk output, and combined decision payload. A higher-level integration check catches field-drop/rename/mapping issues missed by isolated unit tests. I considered System, but rejected it because the behavior text does not explicitly require validating a real external HTTP/UI/export contract; it only requires combined output presence. I rejected Agentic because this behavior checks presence/propagation of fields, not quality of model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the composition layer with real scoring+risk modules (or deterministic outputs from each module interface) and assert the final decision payload contains both `risk_score` and `fraud_probability` plus model-flag attribution fields expected by reviewer-facing consumers.
